# Antigravity Agent — Revit WPF/pyRevit Architectural Directives

**Purpose:** These principles govern all application, UI, state management, theming, and Revit API architectural decisions generated by the Antigravity agent for WPF/pyRevit plugins. Adhere to these directives strictly.

## 1. Architectural & State Management (MVVM)
- **Strict MVVM Separation:** Separate the UI from the underlying data and business logic. 
  - *The View:* Use `UI.xaml` exclusively for WPF layout, controls, and data binding pathways.
  - *The ViewModel:* Use `data_model.py` to manage reactive state and hierarchical data structures.
  - *The Controller/Model:* Use `script.py` to handle Revit API interactions, UI event wiring, and data processing.
- **Reactive State Flow:** Implement the `INotifyPropertyChanged` interface in ViewModel classes. Ensure `OnPropertyChanged` events fire when data changes to automatically update bound XAML elements without direct UI manipulation.
- **Hierarchical Modeling:** Structure data hierarchically (e.g., `MeasurementNode` -> `CategoryNode` -> `FamilyTypeNode` -> `InstanceItem`) to map perfectly to TreeView controls.

## 2. UI/UX Design System & Layout
- **Custom Native Feel:** Build borderless windows (`WindowStyle="None"`) with custom, draggable title bars to create a tailored aesthetic.
- **Split-Pane Navigation:** Utilize a scalable split-pane design using `GridSplitter`. House navigation (TreeView) in the left pane and detail views (DataGrid/contextual headers) in the right pane.
- **Stretch Alignment:** Force list items, TreeView nodes, and DataGrid rows to stretch horizontally (`HorizontalContentAlignment="Stretch"`) to fill available space, maximizing readability and click targets.

### WPF Component Quirks & Tricks
- **DataGrid Selection States:** WPF DataGrids notoriously bake `SystemColors.InactiveSelectionHighlightBrushKey` into their lowest visual trees. To fix dark mode unreadability, bypass XAML triggers entirely and inject the system keys directly into the `Window.Resources` during the Python `apply_theme()` initialization.
- **ComboBox Text Binding:** When binding the `Text="{Binding ...}"` property on a `ComboBox` populated with hardcoded `<ComboBoxItem>` elements, you MUST set `IsEditable="True"`. This forces WPF to hand rendering to the inner TextBox, ensuring the text successfully evaluates and inherits the correct dark mode foreground.

## 3. Theming & Semantic Color System
- **Adaptive Theming:** Programmatically detect the host application's theme (e.g., Revit Light/Dark mode via `is_dark_theme()`) and override the dynamic resources to match.
- **Centralized Dictionary:** Define all UI colors and brushes in a centralized XAML `DynamicResource` dictionary. **Never hardcode hex values directly into component layout styles; always reference the semantic brush keys below.**

### Semantic Brush Dictionary

| Semantic Key | Light Theme (WPF Base) | Dark Theme (Slate/Blue Palette) | Target UI Element |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Backgrounds** | | | |
| `WindowBrush` | `#F3F3F3` | `#1F2937` (Gray-800) | Main application window background |
| `ToolbarBrush` | `#EBF4FA` | `#1F2937` (Gray-800) | Top toolbars and action strips |
| `ControlBrush` | `#FFFFFF` | `#111827` (Gray-900) | Input backgrounds, Grid headers, Left Panes |
| `FooterBrush` | `#EFEFEF` | `#111827` (Gray-900) | Bottom status bars |
| `AltRowBrush` | `#FAFAFA` | `#1F2937` (Gray-800) | Alternating rows in DataGrids |
| **Typography** | | | |
| `TextBrush` | `#333333` | `#F9FAFB` (Gray-50) | Primary text, titles, standard data |
| `TextLightBrush`| `#888888` | `#9CA3AF` (Gray-400) | Secondary text, unit labels, placeholders |
| **Borders & Controls**| | | |
| `BorderBrush` | `#CCCCCC` | `#4B5563` (Gray-600) | Standard borders, grid lines, dividers |
| `ButtonBrush` | `#DDDDDD` | `#374151` (Gray-700) | Default button background |
| `HoverBrush` | `#E5E5E5` | `#374151` (Gray-700) | Button mouse-over state |
| **Accents & Selection**| | | |
| `AccentBrush` | `#0078D7` | `#3B82F6` (Blue-500) | Expanders, active indicators, highlights |
| `SelectionBrush`| `#CCE8FF` | `#1E3A8A` (Blue-900) | Active selected row background |
| `SelectionBorderBrush`| `#0078D7` | `#3B82F6` (Blue-500) | Border of active selected row |
| `SelectionTextBrush`| `Black` | `White` | Text color when an item is selected |
| `InactiveSelectionBrush`| `#E0E0E0` | `#374151` (Gray-700) | Background for selected items lacking focus |
| **Card & Dashboard Specifics**| | | |
| `CardBrush` | `#FFFFFF` | `#374151` (Gray-700) | Dashboard metric cards (elevated) |
| `CardBorderBrush`| `#DCDCDC` | `#4B5563` (Gray-600) | Borders for metric cards |
| `CardTextBrush` | `#000000` | `#FFFFFF` | Primary text inside cards |
| `CardSubTextBrush`| `#666666` | `#D1D5DB` (Gray-300) | Secondary text inside cards |
| `CardLabelBrush`| `#888888` | `#9CA3AF` (Gray-400) | Micro-labels inside cards |
| `CardValueBrush`| `#000000` | `#FFFFFF` | Large value readouts inside cards |
| `CardAccentBrush`| `#0078D7` | `#60A5FA` (Blue-400) | Colored accents or icons inside cards |

## 4. Revit Visualization & Interaction
- **Non-Destructive Highlighting:** When isolating or highlighting elements selected in the UI, do not hide the background. Instead, apply a 60% halftone/transparency to all unselected background elements to maintain spatial context.
- **Element Colorization:** Use `OverrideGraphicSettings` to apply color overrides to element surface and cut patterns.
- **State Persistence:** Cache applied color overrides in an `element_color_map` dictionary. This allows the application to cleanly reset the view to its original state upon request or window close.
- **Optimized API Scanning:** When scanning views, utilize `FilteredElementCollector` and cache category visibility states to minimize redundant Revit API calls.

## 5. Feedback & Observability
- **Color Aggregation:** When visualizing elements, intelligently aggregate colors up the TreeView hierarchy. If a parent node contains children with mixed or varying colors, display a neutral indicator (e.g., Black) to honestly reflect the mixed state.
- **Non-Intrusive Logging:** Use a persistent footer/status bar (`statusLabel`) to display success messages, element counts, or error states without interrupting the user workflow.
- **Contextual Toggles:** Dynamically update the text and enabled-state of actionable buttons based on user selection (e.g., swapping "Isolate" to "Unisolate", or disabling "Export" when no items are checked).
